title: Transform tools: crop UI, resize, skew

tasks.title: Crop mode: dimmed surround, thirds grid, handles, aspect locks, ⏎/⎋
tasks.notes: Core: Crop + CropAspect in PhotonzCore/Crop.swift (19 tests) — fitted/resize/moved/dragRect/thirdsLines, all aspect-locked and canvas-clamped. UI: crop tool enabled (C), full-canvas default rect, marquee selection seeds the rect, handles resize (8pt screen tolerance), inside drag moves, outside drag defines fresh rect, double-click or ⏎ commits (pixel-aligned, one undo step), ⎋ cancels drag then mode. Toolbar shows aspect capsules + ✓/✕ in crop mode (labels need .fixedSize() or SwiftUI collapses them). Chrome: even-odd dim layer, thirds grid, white border, square handles. Verified with in-process harness /tmp/photonz-crop-harness (41 checks, PNGs reviewed). Gotcha: #expect(optional == 4.0/3.0) misreports with bare literal division — use typed constants in tests.

tasks.title: Resize dialog: px/percent, aspect lock, presets, Retina @2x→@1x
tasks.notes: Core: ResizeModel (PhotonzCore, 14 tests) — px/% units with field conversion, aspect lock (re-lock snaps height to width), applyPercent presets, whole-pixel targetSize ≥1×1, isValid/isIdentity. UI: ResizeDialog.swift sheet (segmented px/%, lock toggle, Presets menu incl. Retina @2x→@1x = 50%, live result line, Resize disabled when invalid/identity); toolbar button + Image▸Resize Image… (⌥⌘I). AppState.resizeDocument wraps document.resize in one undo step. Number fields use .grouping(.never) — default format showed '1,200'. Verified in /tmp/photonz-crop-harness T14/T15 (sheet via real screencapture; cacheDisplay misses SwiftUI sheet content).

tasks.title: Per-layer crop (drag inside layer bounds)
tasks.notes: Core: Layer.cropContent(to:) + supportsContentCrop (image layers only — text/annotations re-rasterize at frame size so a stored crop would chase its own tail). Maps a canvas-space sub-rect through the frame→content scale into layer.crop (composes with existing crop) and shrinks the frame, so kept pixels stay put on canvas; renderer pixel test proves it. Crop.resize/moved/dragRect generalized to CGRect bounds (CGSize canvas overloads kept). UI: entering crop with a selected image layer targets it — AppState.cropTargetLayerID + cropBounds (layer frame) confine drags and the dim; commit = one undo step, layer stays selected; undo mid-crop of the target layer falls back to document crop. Verified in /tmp/photonz-crop-harness T16 (pixel-checked two-tone crop).

tasks.title: Skew/rotate UI on selection handles (⌥-drag corners skews, rotate knob)
tasks.notes: Core: TransformDrag (PhotonzCore, 11 tests) — pointerAngle (clockwise-positive, matches LayerTransform), rotation with ⇧ 15° snap, skewed() where the ⌥-dragged corner exactly follows the pointer (tan-additive against half-width/height levers, delta counter-rotated for existing rotation); Layer.transformedCorners for chrome. UI: rotate knob (circle+stem 18 screen pts off the transformed top edge) on non-endpoint layers; ⌥-corner = skew, plain corner = resize; outline+handles draw as the transformed polygon and handle hit-testing maps the pointer through the inverse transform; Esc commits the start transform (History no-op). Sprite preview applies the delta transform (current∘start⁻¹) via CALayer bounds/position+setAffineTransform — never set frame under a transform; transformHold keeps the delta until the post-commit frame lands. Known approximations: sprite is rendered with the start transform baked, so a heavily pre-rotated layer's sprite may clip at its padding box during drags (commit renders correctly); renderSprite has the same clip limit for moves of transformed layers — phase 7 polish note. Verified in /tmp/photonz-crop-harness T17–T19 (90° pixel check, Esc cancel, corner-follows-pointer, plain-resize regression).
